analysis.btc_price_note: BTC ~$77,700 as of Apr 25 12:11 UTC. Flat on day (-0.32% to +0.33%), up ~13% in April (best monthly gain in 1 year). Key resistance $79K-$80K unbroken. Support ~$75K.
sentiment_key_signals: Fear & Greed Index: 46 — highest reading in 3 months, recovering toward Greed
Strategy (MicroStrategy) bought 34,164 BTC for $2.54B — largest purchase since 2024
Spot BTC ETFs: 5-day inflow streak, $1.48B weekly net inflows, AUM $96.5B
KelpDAO $292M exploit — DeFi TVL -$14B to 1-year low; BTC unaffected, altcoins fragile
Technical: 17 bullish vs 12 bearish indicators; 54% bull sentiment
BTC dominance elevated — defensive rotation into BTC from altcoins
FOMC Apr 28-29 upcoming — pre-event compression, vol likely to spike post-meeting
March CPI: 0.9% MoM, >3% YoY — sticky; rate cuts pushed to late 2026
analysis.macro_backdrop: No major US macro data scheduled Apr 25. FOMC Apr 28-29 is next key event — rate hold at 3.50-3.75% is 99.5% priced per Polymarket. Risk: hawkish tone from Warsh (Fed chair nominee stressing independence). GDP Q1 advance + PCE due Apr 30. Big Tech earnings (MSFT/Apple) Apr 29-30. Iran ceasefire extended but fragile. Goldman Sachs filed for new BTC ETF with SEC.
analysis.regime_rationale: Neutral (0.55): April rally (+13%) driven by strong institutional buying (ETF + Strategy) is structurally bullish. However, BTC is compressing just below $79K resistance ahead of FOMC (Apr 28-29). Daily price action flat, F&G still below 50, sticky inflation limits rate cut hopes. Confidence 0.55 (marginally above 0.50 threshold) — macro filter ACTIVE. Bot should respect bearish FOMC tail-risk but lean into institutional-backed dips toward $75K-$76K.
